notes: Batons are generally treated as weapons in Japan and cannot be carried by civilians without a valid reason.
Kubotans may be legal as simple keychains but carrying them for use as a weapon can be illegal
Self-defence keychains may be legal if they are ordinary items, but weaponised versions can be treated as illegal weapons.
Carrying a sword in public in Japan is highly restricted and generally only allowed for legitimate purposes such as transport or cultural use.
Tasers are prohibited in Japan and are not legal for civilian ownership or carry.
Pepper spray is not explicitly banned in Japan but carrying it for self-defence can create legal issues
Switchblades are illegal in Japan and are classified as prohibited knives.
Butterfly knives are illegal in Japan and fall under prohibited knife categories.
Stun guns are generally prohibited in Japan and are considered illegal weapons for civilian possession.
Brass knuckles are illegal in Japan and are treated as prohibited weapons.
